channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace and NATO membership
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Sunday he would be ready to give up the presidency if doing so would achieve a lasting peace for his country under the security umbrella of the NATO military alliance.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli tanks move into the occupied West Bank for the first time since 2002 amid growing crackdown
item.description: Israeli tanks have moved into the occupied West Bank for the first time since 2002, shortly after the defense minister said troops will remain “for the coming year” in parts of the territory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis is conscious and receiving supplemental oxygen following a respiratory crisis
item.description: The Vatican says Pope Francis is still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen after doctors earlier said that the 88-year-old pope, who had part of one lung removed as a young man, was in critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after Taliban lift suspension
item.description: The Taliban say an Afghan women’s radio station will resume broadcasts after its operations were suspended over alleged cooperation with an overseas TV channel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wounded, recovered and back to war. Ukrainian soldiers are returning to battle after amputation
item.description: Ukrainian soldiers who lost limbs in the three-year war against Russia are returning to the battlefield out of a sense of duty amid the grim outlook for their country.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, Evgeniy Maloletka And Vasilisa Stepanenko, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel delays Palestinian prisoner release after Hamas' 'humiliating' treatment of hostages, Netanyahu says
item.description: The seventh hostage-prisoner exchange between Israel and Hamas has been delayed due to the terrorists' "humiliating" treatment of hostages, according to Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope Francis' health 'looking very serious': Vatican reporter
item.description: When journalist Colm Flynn last saw Pope Francis, the Holy Father was “in good spirits” but visibly “struggling to catch his breath.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Isolated in 'harsh conditions:' Deportee from US details legal limbo in Panama camp near Darien Gap
item.description: CARTI, Panama (AP) — A deportee from the United States detained in a camp in rural Panama, among a hundred who refused to return to their countries, described on Saturday waiting in limbo under “harsh conditions” and cut off from access to legal council and other rights.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sununu urges Zelensky to 'be careful' not to anger Trump
item.description: Former New Hampshire Gov. Chris Sununu (R) advised Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during a recent interview to “be careful” not to anger President Trump. His warning comes as the two leaders have gone tit-for-tat in recent days after U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Thousands rally in Romania's capital to support far-right Georgescu after annulled presidential race
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Thousands of protesters took to the streets of Romania’s capital on Saturday in a show of support for Călin Georgescu, the far-right populist who emerged as the front-runner in last year’s presidential race that was annulled days before the second-round runoff.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 70 Christians beheaded in African country by ISIS-aligned militants, groups say; world mostly silent
item.description: Reports indicate that 70 Christians were beheaded by Islamic militants in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The group that killed the faithful is an affiliate of the Islamic state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas releases more hostages in exchange for more than 600 Palestinian prisoners as part of ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas freed more hostages on Saturday in exchange for more than 600 Palestinian prisoners as part of the delicate ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of Shiri Bibas positively identified after eventually being handed over by Hamas
item.description: The body of a woman who was presumed to have been one of four dead hostages Hamas handed over to Israel was positively identified Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of Shiri Bibas, mom of two killed, allegedly returned to Israel following Hamas' broken promise
item.description: The remains of a woman killed while in captivity by Hamas was supposedly handed over to Israel on Friday, a day after her body was supposed to be brought back.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sinn Féin leaders won't attend St. Patrick's event at White House to protest US stance on Gaza
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sinn Féin's leaders said Friday they will not attend a traditional St. Patrick's Day event next month at the White House as a protest against President Donald Trump's stance on Gaza.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Zelensky 'has no cards,' 'shouldn't be at meetings' with Russia
item.description: President Trump lashed out at Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday, saying he did not think it was important for Zelensky to attend negotiations about the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Democrat: Europe worried Czech Republic, Poland, Baltic states could be next for Putin
item.description: Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) expressed concern that Russian President Vladimir Putin and other authoritarian leaders will be emboldened by the United States' shift on the Ukraine-Russia war under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: European troops, US support are part of an emerging plan for Ukraine. But it faces many hurdles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As the U.S. and Russia push ahead — so far without Ukraine at the table – on talks to end the war, political and military leaders in Europe are fleshing out details of a plan for European forces to help ensure Moscow doesn't attack again.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s attempts to denigrate Zelenskyy have led to a surge in Ukrainian unity
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Days before the third anniversary of Russia's full-scale invasion, Ukrainians are as somber and tense as they were right before Moscow launched the war. Only now, they aren’t just worried about their longtime enemy.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK foreign secretary questions Russia's 'appetite' for peace and challenges Lavrov at tense G20 meet
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — U.K. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said he saw no appetite for peace from Russia in Ukraine after listening to a speech by Russia's top diplomat at a tense Group of 20 meeting in South Africa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, MICHELLE GUMEDE and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Facing pressure from Trump, Costa Rica and Honduras join Panama as stopovers for foreign deportees
item.description: SAN JOSÉ, Costa Rica (AP) — A group of families and children hailing from Uzbekistan, China, Afghanistan, Russia and more countries climbed down the stairs of an airplane in Costa Rica's capital Thursday, the first flight of deportees from other nations Costa Rica agreed to hold in detention facilit
item.dc:creator: JAVIER CÓRDOBA and MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Remains of young mom Shiri Bibas, taken hostage and killed by Hamas, not returned despite promise, Israel says
item.description: The mother of a woman abducted with her two children and husband was not among the slain hostages returned to Israel by Hamas on Thursday, Israel said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's UN ambassador slams Hamas' 'evil and depraved' display of hostages' coffins
item.description: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk condemned Hamas' parading of the coffins containing Shiri, Ariel and Kfir Bibas and Oded Lifshitz.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Terror attack rocks Israel; three buses reportedly explode
item.description: Israel officials have ordered all bus and train services halted while all vehicles are inspected for bombs following two bus explosions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump warns Zelenskyy to quickly negotiate war's end with Russia or risk not having a nation to lead
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that he “better move fast” to negotiate an end to Russia's invasion of Ukraine or risk not having a nation to lead.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MIKE PESOLI and STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: "We found that more than 5 billion people—ie, more than 60% of the world's population—do not have access to safe, quality, and affordable medical oxygen services," the commission wrote.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Palestinians should be able to leave Gaza if they choose: Israel envoy
item.description: Hamas is speeding up the hostage exchange with Israel, announcing six people will be released Saturday instead of three.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Tourist in Turks and Caicos severely injured in shark attack
item.description: “While on what was supposed to be a relaxing vacation, my sister-in-law and her husband, my brother, lived what can only be described as a nightmare,” Al Chevarie,said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Delta plane crash: Passengers live after escaping ‘upside down’ jet
item.description: Air traffic control audio captured the moment officials realized Delta Flight 4819 was burning on the tarmac.
item.dc:creator: Alex Caprariello, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: US leaders in Saudi Arabia for peace talks on Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he will not accept a deal without a seat at the negotiating table.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation
